failure_scenario: switchAccountInTab() crashes with 'RESPONSE_INTERCEPTOR is not defined', breaking zero-memory-spike tab reuse feature. Should be INTERCEPTOR_SCRIPT (defined at line 127).
impact: Tab switching feature completely broken, forces browser restart on account exhaustion

summary: Time unit mismatch: stores milliseconds, Python expects seconds
failure_scenario: Sets credits_check_after = Date.now() + 24h_in_ms (milliseconds), but Python proxy.py:84 compares with time.time() (seconds). Account locked out for 1000x longer: ~3 years instead of 24 hours.
impact: Pool mode accounts become unusable for years after first credit exhaustion

failure_scenario: Concurrent switches do separate delete() and add() operations without lock. Two requests can both see account X available, both switch to it, violating single-session-per-account constraint.
impact: Multiple browsers logged into same account trigger security alerts, account bans

summary: Duplicate account switching logic (NO_CREDITS vs QUOTA_EXCEEDED)
failure_scenario: 50+ line blocks at 844-900 and 902-940 are nearly identical. Changes to switching logic must be duplicated, increasing bug surface area.
impact: Maintenance burden, high risk of inconsistent behavior between error paths

summary: Write amplification: full config write on every request
failure_scenario: _mark_used() → _persist_account() → config_manager.update_account() writes entire config.json to disk on every API call. At 10 req/s with 2 accounts, that's 864,000 disk writes per day.
impact: SSD wear, I/O contention, config corruption risk under concurrent writes

summary: Redundant config reloads in tight loop
failure_scenario: loadConfig() called inside runAccount loop (every 60s per account) and getAvailableAccounts (multiple times per cycle). Reads full config.json from disk repeatedly instead of watching for changes.
impact: Unnecessary I/O, stale config reads if external changes occur mid-cycle

summary: Code duplication: getAvailableAccounts() duplicated across files
failure_scenario: getAvailableAccounts() defined in both lazy_server.js:978 and session_daemon.js:411 with nearly identical logic. Bug fixes must be applied twice.
impact: Maintenance burden, risk of logic divergence between pool and lazy modes

summary: Dead variable: fresh_acct assigned but never used
failure_scenario: fresh_acct = None assigned at line 114 but never read. Dead code that adds no value.

summary: Unused field: Account.current_concurrent not persisted or used
failure_scenario: Account dataclass has current_concurrent field but it's excluded from to_dict() (line 70 comment). Runtime tracking uses separate _account_concurrent dict in proxy.py instead.
impact: Dead field creates confusion about concurrency tracking design

summary: Time unit inconsistency between lazy and pool modes
failure_scenario: Pool mode (session_daemon.js:413) uses Date.now() (milliseconds) for comparison, lazy mode (lazy_server.js:981) uses Math.floor(Date.now()/1000) (seconds). Both work internally but create confusion.
